name: The English Opening
description: Line trainer for the English Opening, generated from the canonical opening database (lichess-org/chess-openings): 66 positions drilled book-move by book-move from White's side across 175 named variations. Each card shows the position from White's side and asks for the book move; the back gives the line so far, the variation name and ECO code, and the covered replies that continue it.

fields.prompt: You are White in the main line. What is the book move?
fields.extra: Line: 1. **c4**. This is the main line (A10). Covered replies: 1... e5 (King's English Variation), 1... e6 (Agincourt Defense), 1... Nf6 (Anglo-Indian Defense), 1... c5 (Symmetrical Variation), 1... f5 (Anglo-Dutch Defense), 1... d5 (Anglo-Scandinavian Defense), 1... g6 (Great Snake Variation), 1... g5 (Myers Defense), 1... Nc6 (Anglo-Lithuanian Variation), 1... b5 (Jaenisch Gambit), 1... c6 (Caro-Kann Defensive System).

fields.prompt: You are White in the King's English Variation. What is the book move?
fields.extra: Line: 1. c4 e5 2. **Nc3**. This is the King's English Variation, Reversed Sicilian (A21). Covered replies: 2... Nf6 (King's English Variation, Two Knights Variation), 2... Nc6 (King's English Variation, Reversed Closed Sicilian), 2... d6, 2... Bb4 (King's English Variation, Kramnik-Shirov Counterattack).
